sprint.name: Two starter tickets generated by `koru --init`. Replace them with

STARTER-001.description:
Verify the koru ↔ planfile ↔ LLM loop end-to-end on this project.

1. Run `koru` (no args) and read the markdown brief.
2. Confirm the policy table shows all gates as `False`.
3. Confirm `STARTER-001` is the active ticket.
4. Run `planfile ticket complete STARTER-001 --note "wired"`.
STARTER-001.name: Confirm koru is wired correctly

STARTER-002.description:
Edit `.planfile/sprints/current.yaml` and add the work that
actually matters for this project. You can also re-run
`koru --init --force --from your-pipeline.yaml` to import a
flat pipeline you already maintain elsewhere.
STARTER-002.name: Replace starter tickets with your real backlog

STARTER-012.description: BLOCKED: Working-tree refactor is complete and verified: regix compare HEAD --local reports '0 errors, 0 warnings, 96 improvements; Gates: PASS'. radon MI >= 20 and lizard CC < 16 for every module/function in goal/. task test: 302 passed, 2 skipped. New modules: goal/bootstrap/costs_badge.py, goal/bootstrap/pyproject_costs_setup.py, goal/deep_analyzer_patterns.py, goal/deep_analyzer_aggregate.py, goal/doctor/python_diag_core.py, goal/doctor/python_diag_extended.py, goal/smart_commit/generator_core.py, goal/smart_commit/generator_generate.py, goal/cli/tests_discovery.py, goal/cli/tests_pytest_setup.py. Cannot mark done: regix gates reads git HEAD and the changes are uncommitted; koru policy allow_commit=False forbids me from committing. Needs human/CI to: 1) git commit -a, 2) bash scripts/koru_verify_ci.sh (will be exit 0), 3) planfile ticket done STARTER-012, 4) planfile ticket update STARTER-011 --status open && planfile ticket done STARTER-011.

STARTER-013.description: TestQL auto-generated scenarios pull pytest 'assert' statements from arbitrary sources (including .venv site-packages: gitdb, smmap, pandas, jsonschema), producing 52 invalid scenarios. Generator must (a) only scan project tests/ tree, (b) ignore asserts whose operands are not plain literals/identifiers (e.g. str(expr), schema["$id"], obj.method() calls), (c) skip empty-RHS forms. Acceptance: task scenario-run testql-scenarios/generated-from-pytests.testql.toon.yaml has 0 errors; total ASSERT count >= 10 and all rows pass shape validation.
STARTER-013.name: Fix TestQL scenario generator: filter out non-target asserts

STARTER-014.description: 2026-07-03: goal printed 'Running tests in 5 subproject(s)' then 'Tests failed. Aborting' with every visible subproject log green — no culprit named. Print 'failed in: PATH (exit N)' plus the failing runner's tail. Cost: an hour of manual bisection per incident.
STARTER-014.name: Name the failing subproject in test aggregation

notes: Fixed run_tests()'s bare except Exception: success=False silently swallowing pre-subprocess exceptions with zero diagnostic (goal/cli/tests.py). Now prints failed-in message naming project type and exception. 2 new tests, full suite (397) passes.

STARTER-015.description: goal commits a snapshot of a tree that concurrent agents/loops may be editing mid-run (2026-07-03: commit a498ff9 in semcod/koru landed with 19 red tests authored mid-flight by another process). Compare git status hash before/after the test phase; on drift warn and re-run or skip commit.
STARTER-015.name: Tree-stability check around test/commit phases

STARTER-016.description: goal/recovery/manager.py auto-stash guard (commit in repo, tests/test_recovery_preserves_work.py) is unpublished; PyPI 2.1.264 still destroys uncommitted work on recovery reset --hard. Bump + publish.
STARTER-016.name: Release: publish reset-hard auto-stash fix
